retry_reason: Failure (return NULL) indicates no controlling tty or no signal struct; this is a process property that will not change on retry.
contract_summary: Get wrapper: safely acquires a reference to the current process's controlling tty_struct (if any) and returns it to the caller. The caller owns the reference and must call tty_kref_put() to release it when done. The function takes the sighand siglock briefly to protect the read of current->signal->tty, but does not require the caller to hold any lock. If no controlling tty exists (tty pointer NULL), returns NULL; the caller must check the return value before use.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to safely acquire a reference to the current process's controlling tty via tty_kref_get and return it; minimal non-refcount logic (spinlock and null check) is just infrastructure for safe reference counting.

check.reason: tty_kref_get is called on current->signal->tty, and the result is returned. The returned tty is the critical variable. The access path tty.kref.refcount and type chain tty_struct--kref--refcount_t match the callee's definition and the code.
